title: Clamp value to maximum using xor/mul — branch-free min(value, max)
description: xor(v, mul(xor(v, max), lt(max, v))) computes min(v, max) without a JUMPI. When v <= max: lt=0, mul=0, result=v. When v > max: lt=1, mul=xor(v,max), outer xor cancels to max. Three opcodes (XOR, MUL, LT) replace a conditional branch.
pattern_after:
// Branchless min(index, length)
index := xor(index, mul(xor(index, length), lt(length, index)))
when_to_apply: Any min(a, b) computation where both values are uint256 and silent clamping is acceptable
when_not_to_apply: When out-of-bounds should revert, when one value is signed
